articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5 in early April 2026, featuring natively fused vision, audio, and text reasoning in a single architecture rather than bolted-on adapters. The model demonstrates significant gains on agentic coding benchmarks and supports a 1M-token context window with improved recall, positioning it as a step toward more reliable autonomous AI workflows.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Ultra 2 Achieves State-of-the-Art on Scientific Reasoning Benchmarks
articles.summary: DeepMind released Gemini Ultra 2, which topped GPQA-Diamond and new graduate-level STEM benchmarks by meaningful margins over prior frontier models. The model integrates a chain-of-thought verification loop that significantly reduces hallucination on quantitative problems, making it particularly relevant for research copilot applications.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Begins Commercial Deployment of Humanoid Workers at BMW Factories
articles.summary: Figure AI announced the first paid commercial deployment of its Figure 02 humanoid robots at BMW's Spartanburg plant, handling bin-picking, part inspection, and kit staging across three production lines. The deployment marks a milestone as one of the first revenue-generating general-purpose humanoid robotics contracts, with per-unit costs reportedly competitive with three-shift human labor over a 30-month horizon.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Electric Demonstrates Autonomous Tool Use in Unstructured Environments
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics released video and technical details of its electric Atlas performing autonomous tool manipulation — operating drills, turning valves, and carrying irregular loads — in a simulated disaster response scenario. The system leverages a vision-language-action model fine-tuned with reinforcement learning, achieving 94% task completion without teleoperation on previously unseen configurations.

articles.title: Meta Releases Llama 4 Behemoth as Open-Weight Model with MoE Architecture
articles.summary: Meta released the full Llama 4 Behemoth mixture-of-experts model with open weights, featuring 288 expert modules totaling over 1.5 trillion parameters but activating roughly 200B per forward pass. The release includes a commercially permissive license and represents the most capable open-weight model to date, narrowing the gap with proprietary frontier systems on major benchmarks.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Risk Production of 1.4nm A14 Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed risk production has started on its A14 (1.4nm-class) process node at its Fab 20 facility in Hsinchu, approximately one quarter ahead of the original roadmap. The node uses gate-all-around nanosheet transistors with backside power delivery, promising a 15% speed improvement or 30% power reduction over N2, and is expected to power next-generation AI accelerators starting in late 2027.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface Trial
articles.summary: The FDA cleared Neuralink's N2 implant for a second-phase human clinical trial involving 30 patients with quadriplegia. The updated device doubles electrode count to 2,048 channels and introduces bidirectional stimulation, enabling preliminary sensory feedback alongside motor decoding — a critical step toward closed-loop brain-computer interface systems.

articles.title: Toyota Research Institute Demonstrates Diffusion-Policy-Based Dexterous Manipulation Across 60+ Household Tasks
articles.summary: TRI published results from its large-scale behavior cloning program, where a single diffusion policy model trained on teleoperated demonstrations successfully generalized to over 60 household manipulation tasks including folding, pouring, and drawer organization. The work demonstrates that scaling demonstration data with consistent hardware can yield broadly capable robotic manipulation without per-task reward engineering.
